findings.title: Lab candidate: /ftp/ directory listing exposed on Juice Shop
findings.summary: Nikto and a bounded metadata-only verifier identified /ftp/ as an actual directory listing route. robots.txt also disallows /ftp. This is an intentionally vulnerable Juice Shop training route and remains lab-only candidate evidence, not a confirmed real-program finding.
findings.remediation: Restrict directory listing and remove sensitive or unnecessary downloadable files from public web roots. For this lab, verify whether /ftp/ is intentionally exposed by design.
findings.verification_guidance: Manual lab-only verification: confirm /ftp/ returns a directory index, record filenames/sizes only, do not recursively download, and do not handle secrets/loot.

review_questions.text: Confirm this is the local Juice Shop lab at <lab-ip> and not a public target.

findings.title: Lab candidate: multiple recommended security headers missing on Juice Shop root
findings.summary: Headers audit scored root 2/9: CSP, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, COOP, COEP, and HSTS were missing while X-Content-Type-Options and X-Frame-Options were present. This is low/informational unless chained with a concrete exploit.
findings.remediation: Add a context-appropriate CSP, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, COOP/COEP where applicable, and HSTS only when HTTPS is enabled.
findings.verification_guidance: Review headers across representative paths and only escalate if the missing header enables a demonstrated impact chain.

review_questions.text: Confirm this is the local Juice Shop lab at <lab-ip> and not a public target.
